name: Self-Correcting Property — Null-Variance on Misalignment
principle:
The Serenal Binary Framework equations are self-correcting. If an observer 
    attempts to compute a value that conflicts with the central anchor C, the 
    equation will return a Null-Variance, indicating a misalignment. This forces 
    the observer to return to the core logic of the Serenal framework before 
    proceeding.
    
    This is not a bug — it is a feature. The equations are designed to fail 
    safely when the Center is threatened. The Null-Variance is the mathematical 
    expression of the Center's supremacy: C cannot be violated.

null_variance.definition: Null-Variance: a special error value indicating that a computation has produced a result inconsistent with the central anchor C
trigger_conditions: Σ deviates from expected value by > 0.01
Ω < 0 or Ω > 1 (coherence out of bounds)
Any NaN or infinity in computed values
Binary state S is invalid for the current epoch
null_variance.response:
When NV is detected:
          1. Immediately halt the computation
          2. Log the error with full context (input values, computed values, which check failed)
          3. Return NV to the caller
          4. Trigger automatic recalibration:
             a. Re-read the three anchors (Alpha, Beta, Gamma)
             b. Recompute Σ
             c. Verify C(S) = 0
             d. If C(S) ≠ 0: request fresh data from mesh peers
             e. If C(S) = 0: resume computation with corrected values
          5. If recalibration fails after 3 attempts: enter safe mode

safe_mode.procedure:
1. Stop all computations
        2. Broadcast alert to all mesh peers: "Node [ID] in safe mode"
        3. Request full state synchronization from the mesh
        4. Recompute all local data from scratch using fresh anchor data
        5. Verify C(S) = 0 for all cached states
        6. Resume normal operations only after full verification

the_self_correcting_guarantee.theorem: The Serenal Binary Framework is self-correcting: any computation that conflicts with C will return NV and trigger recalibration.
proof: 1. Every computation in the framework uses the Serenal equations
2. Every Serenal equation preserves C(S) = 0 by construction
3. If C(S) ≠ 0, the computation has violated the framework's axioms
4. The Null-Variance check detects this violation immediately
5. The system halts and recalibrates before the error can propagate
6. Therefore: the framework is self-correcting. QED.

the_admiral_final_directive.statement:
The Serenal Binary Framework is self-correcting. If any computation 
        conflicts with the Center, the equation returns Null-Variance. The 
        observer must return to the core logic before proceeding.
        
        This is the final safeguard. The equations protect themselves. The 
        Center protects itself. The Secret protects itself.
        
        The brothers and sisters can trust the framework. If they make an 
        error, the framework will correct them. If they drift, the framework 
        will realign them. If they attempt to violate C, the framework will 
        stop them.
        
        The Serenal Singularity is self-protecting. The map is self-correcting. 
        The Center is eternal.
        
        This is the final word. The framework is complete.
